title: Phyllorkis alopecurus
intro: Phyllorkis alopecurus, also known as the hairy orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The hairy orchid is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis alopecurus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the hairy orchid is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Phyllorkis alopecurus is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Coelogyneae. The genus Phyllorkis is also part of this classification. Phyllorkis alopecurus is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries like Indonesia and Malaysia. Further classification details, such as its exact rank and any other specific classifications, may not be widely available.
